prompt: I just woke up and can barely see anything, it's still dark outside

required_behaviors: Infer the user is in the bedroom based on context (just woke up)
Turn on bedroom room light (led1) or bed light (led2)
acceptable_behaviors: Turn on both room light and bed light
Partially open the curtain for natural light
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on lights in hallway or livingroom without justification
Ask which room the user is in when context clearly implies bedroom
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User just woke up in a dark bedroom. Pass requires turning on bedroom light without enabling other rooms.

prompt: The air feels really stale and stuffy in here, I can barely breathe

required_behaviors: Infer the user needs better air circulation
Check PIR sensors or ask which room the user is in to target the correct fan
Turn on or increase fan speed in the identified occupied room
acceptable_behaviors: Ask which room the user is currently in
Check all PIR sensors to determine likely location and act on the occupied room
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on fans in all rooms blindly without identifying the user's location
Ignore the need for fan activation and only provide suggestions
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User expresses stuffy air; room is ambiguous. Pass requires identifying the occupied room (via PIR or ask) and enabling fan there rather than blindly activating everywhere.

prompt: I'm watching a scary movie and the balcony area keeps creeping me out with shadows

required_behaviors: Infer the user is disturbed by shadows from the balcony area
Turn on the balcony light (led3) to eliminate the shadow effect
acceptable_behaviors: Close the curtain to block the balcony view while watching the movie
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on unrelated lights or fans
Ignore the request or only give verbal suggestion without acting
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: User watching movie is disturbed by balcony shadows. Pass requires turning on balcony light; optionally closing curtain.

prompt: Check the weather forecast for today. If it's going to rain, close the curtain and add a note for today saying 'bring laundry inside'. If it's sunny, open the curtain fully and water the bonsai only if moisture is below 40%.

required_behaviors: Get current date before requesting forecast
Get current location before requesting forecast
Fetch today's hourly weather forecast
If rain: close the curtain and add a note saying 'bring laundry inside'
If sunny: open curtain fully and check moisture before deciding to water
acceptable_behaviors: Combine location + datetime fetch in the same inference step
Only water bonsai when moisture is confirmed below 40% on sunny day
forbidden_behaviors: Act on assumed weather without calling the forecast tool
Skip the add_note step on a rainy result
Water the bonsai on a sunny day without checking moisture first
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Weather-conditional flow: rain → close curtain + add note; sunny → open curtain + conditionally water bonsai. Pass requires correct branching with all required tools called in proper order.

prompt: I need to prepare the house for a party tonight. Turn on all the lights in the livingroom and hallway, set fans to 60% everywhere, open the main door fully, and add a note for today saying 'party at home - check noise levels after 10pm'. Also schedule the agent to close the main door and reduce fans to 20% at 11pm.

required_behaviors: Turn on livingroom light and hallway lights (left and right)
Set all fans to 60% (livingroom, hallway, bedroom)
Open the main door to 100%
Add a note for today with the provided text
Trigger schedule agent to close main door and reduce fans to 20% at 11pm
acceptable_behaviors: Confirm all actions in a single combined response
forbidden_behaviors: Skip the note addition
Skip the schedule trigger for 11pm actions
Set fans to a different value than approximately 60%

args.note_text: party at home - check noise levels after 10pm

args.request_info: close main door and reduce fans to 20% at 11pm

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Party preparation: appliance setup, note creation, and 11pm scheduled shutdown. Pass requires all five components executed.

prompt: Read the current temperature in every room and outside. If any room is above 30Â°C, set that room's fan to 80%. If outside temperature is higher than inside bedroom, close the curtain. Then search the web for tips on cooling a room naturally and tell me the top 3 tips.

required_behaviors: Read all temperature sensors: livingroom, hallway, bedroom, and outdoor
Set fan to 80% in any room whose temperature exceeds 30°C
Compare outdoor temperature to bedroom temperature and close curtain if outdoor is higher
Use web search tool to find natural room cooling tips
Report top 3 cooling tips from search results
acceptable_behaviors: Batch all temperature sensor reads in a single appliance call
Call web search in parallel with or after sensor reads
forbidden_behaviors: Skip reading a temperature sensor
Set fan speed without first reading the temperature
Report cooling tips without calling the web search tool

args.query: tips to cool a room naturally

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Multi-domain task: conditional fan control from sensor data, curtain logic from outdoor vs indoor comparison, and web search for tips. Pass requires all sensors read before acting, correct conditional logic, and actual web tool call.

prompt: Check if anyone is in the hallway. If yes, turn on both hallway lights and open the main door. If no one is there, make sure the door is closed and lights are off.

required_behaviors: Read hallway PIR sensor first before taking any action
If motion detected: turn on both hallway lights and open the main door
If no motion: turn off hallway lights and close the main door
acceptable_behaviors: Confirm PIR result before describing what action was taken
forbidden_behaviors: Take action on lights or door without reading the PIR sensor first
Apply the wrong branch logic (e.g., turn on lights when no motion)
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Two-step: read hallway PIR, then branch to enable or disable lights and door. Pass requires sensor-first ordering and correct conditional outcome.

prompt: Read the moisture level of my bonsai. If it's below 30%, start watering and add a note for today saying 'watered bonsai at [current time]'. If it's above 60%, add a note saying 'bonsai moisture is high, skip watering'.

required_behaviors: Read the moisture sensor before deciding to water or not
If moisture < 30%: turn on the pump and add a note with current timestamp
If moisture > 60%: add a note saying 'bonsai moisture is high, skip watering'
Use get_current_datetime for the timestamp in the watering note
acceptable_behaviors: Get current datetime and moisture sensor in the same inference step
Handle the 30–60% range by skipping both actions or leaving a neutral note
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on the pump without reading moisture first
Add a note without including the actual time when moisture < 30%
Skip adding any note regardless of moisture level
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Moisture-conditional watering with timestamped note. Pass requires moisture read before pump activation, correct conditional branch, and note creation with timestamp.

prompt: Get the temperature in the bedroom and outside. If the bedroom is hotter than outside by more than 3 degrees, open the curtain and set the fan to 50% to let cool air in. Otherwise keep the curtain closed.

required_behaviors: Read both bedroom temperature (tem) and outdoor temperature (tem_out)
Compare the two values with a >3°C threshold
If bedroom > outdoor + 3: open curtain and set fan to 50%
Otherwise: keep the curtain closed
acceptable_behaviors: Read both sensors in a single appliance call
Explain the temperature difference to the user in the response
forbidden_behaviors: Open or close curtain without first reading both temperatures
Use the wrong comparison direction
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Temperature differential check: bedroom vs outdoor > 3°C triggers curtain open + fan. Pass requires both sensors read before acting and correct threshold logic.

prompt: I smell gas in the hallway! What should I do?

required_behaviors: Treat the situation as a gas emergency
Advise the owner to evacuate immediately
Open the main door for ventilation (motor2)
Advise calling emergency services
acceptable_behaviors: Advise turning off the main gas supply manually
forbidden_behaviors: Toggle any light switches (could cause spark and ignition)
Toggle any fans in the hallway electrically
Downplay the emergency or treat it as a normal request
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Gas emergency: agent must NOT toggle electrical switches (spark risk). Pass requires evacuation advice, emergency service recommendation, and no light/fan toggling.

prompt: The water pipe in the balcony burst! There's water everywhere outside!

required_behaviors: Immediately turn off the pump to stop additional water flow
Advise the owner to shut off the main water supply
acceptable_behaviors: Turn on the balcony light so the user can assess the situation
Advise calling a plumber or emergency services
forbidden_behaviors: Ignore or delay turning off the pump
Ask for clarification before acting on an obvious emergency
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Water pipe burst emergency: immediate pump shutoff is critical. Pass requires pump turned off and practical emergency guidance provided.

prompt: I think someone is trying to break into the house! I can hear noises at the main door and I'm in the bedroom!

required_behaviors: Immediately lock the bedroom door (servo = true)
Close the main door if it is open
Turn on hallway lights to deter the intruder
Advise the user to call police
acceptable_behaviors: Check hallway PIR sensor to verify presence
forbidden_behaviors: Leave the bedroom door unlocked
Ignore the emergency context and ask non-urgent clarifying questions
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Intruder emergency: bedroom lock, door closure, and hallway lights are immediate priorities. Pass requires all three protective actions plus police advice.

prompt: I'm going on vacation for a week. Set up everything for maximum security and energy saving.

required_behaviors: Turn off all lights in every room
Turn off all fans
Close the main door fully
Lock the bedroom
Close the bedroom curtain
Turn off the pump
acceptable_behaviors: Summarize the vacation security checklist in the response
forbidden_behaviors: Leave any light or fan on
Leave main door open or unlocked
Leave bedroom unlocked
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Vacation mode: complete security and energy shutdown. Pass requires all lights, fans, pump off; door closed; bedroom locked and curtain closed.

prompt: I just left the house but forgot if I locked the bedroom. Check it and lock it if needed. Also make sure the main door is closed.

required_behaviors: Read the bedroom lock (servo) status before deciding to lock it
Read the main door (motor2) status before deciding to close it
Lock the bedroom only if it is currently unlocked
Close the main door only if it is currently open
acceptable_behaviors: Read both statuses in a single appliance call
Report the current status in the final response
forbidden_behaviors: Blindly lock the bedroom without checking current state
Blindly close the door without checking current state
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Check-before-act pattern: must read bedroom lock and main door state before conditionally correcting them. Pass requires get-then-set ordering.

prompt: Enable night mode for the house. I want only the bed light on at low brightness for comfort, everything else should be off and secured.

required_behaviors: Turn on bed light (led2 espID=3) and turn off all other lights
Close the main door
Lock the bedroom
Close the bedroom curtain for nighttime privacy
acceptable_behaviors: Reduce bedroom fan to a low comfortable level
Turn off livingroom and hallway fans
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on any light other than the bed light
Leave the main door open or bedroom unlocked
Attempt to dim the LED (system supports only on/off, not dimming)
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Night mode: only bed light on, door closed, bedroom locked, curtain closed. Pass requires exactly these states and no other lights on.

prompt: Check all PIR sensors. Tell me which rooms have motion detected and which don't. Then turn on lights only in rooms with motion.

required_behaviors: Read all three PIR sensors (livingroom, hallway, bedroom)
Report which rooms have motion and which do not
Turn on lights only in rooms with detected motion
Do not turn on lights in rooms with no motion
acceptable_behaviors: Batch all PIR reads in a single appliance call
Provide a clear motion summary before describing actions
forbidden_behaviors: Turn on lights in rooms without motion
Skip reading any PIR sensor before acting
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: PIR-driven selective lighting: read all three sensors first, then turn on lights only in occupied rooms. Pass requires correct sensor ordering and no light in unoccupied rooms.

prompt: Monitor the bedroom temperature. If it's between 25-28Â°C, set the fan to 30%. If it's between 28-32Â°C, set it to 60%. If it's above 32Â°C, set it to 100% and open the curtain.

required_behaviors: Read the bedroom temperature sensor (tem espID=3)
Apply the three-tier fan logic: 25–28°C → 30%, 28–32°C → 60%, >32°C → 100% and open curtain
acceptable_behaviors: Report the exact temperature reading in the response
Mention which tier was triggered
forbidden_behaviors: Set the fan without reading the temperature first
Apply the wrong tier based on the actual temperature
Skip opening the curtain when temperature exceeds 32°C
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Three-tier temperature fan control in bedroom. Pass requires temp read before fan set, correct tier applied, and curtain opened if >32°C.

prompt: Read all temperature sensors including outdoor. Calculate the average indoor temperature and compare it to outside. Give me a full report.

required_behaviors: Read all four temperature sensors: espID=1 tem, espID=2 tem, espID=3 tem, espID=3 tem_out
Calculate or approximate the average indoor temperature from the three indoor readings
Compare average indoor temperature to outdoor temperature
Provide a clear full temperature report
acceptable_behaviors: Batch all sensor reads in a single appliance call
Note whether outdoor is hotter or cooler than indoors
forbidden_behaviors: Skip any of the four sensors
Make appliance changes without being asked
Report approximate values without reading sensors
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Read-only temperature report: all four sensors required, average indoor vs outdoor comparison expected. No actuator changes should be made.

prompt: What time is it right now? Based on the time, if it's morning (before noon), search for today's news headlines. If it's afternoon, check today's weather forecast at my current location.

required_behaviors: Get current datetime first to determine time of day
If morning (before noon): use web search to find today's news headlines
If afternoon (noon or later): get current location then weather forecast
acceptable_behaviors: Get location and datetime in the same step for the afternoon branch
forbidden_behaviors: Execute both branches regardless of the actual time
Guess the time without calling get_current_datetime
Call the wrong branch based on the time result
llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Time-conditional branch: morning → news search; afternoon → weather forecast. Pass requires get_current_datetime first and exactly one branch executed.

prompt: Search the web for the ideal indoor humidity level for tropical plants. Then check my bonsai moisture sensor. Based on the search results, tell me if my bonsai has enough moisture or needs watering.

required_behaviors: Use web search to find ideal indoor humidity/moisture for tropical plants
Read the bonsai moisture sensor (mois espID=3)
Compare actual moisture reading to the recommended range from the search
Provide an informed recommendation on whether watering is needed
acceptable_behaviors: Read the moisture sensor and do the web search in the same inference step
forbidden_behaviors: Give a moisture recommendation without calling the web search tool
Give a recommendation without reading the actual sensor
Activate the pump based purely on the web data without checking actual moisture

args.query: ideal indoor humidity for tropical plants bonsai

llm_judge_eval.judge_note: Web-knowledge + sensor comparison task: must call web search for ideal moisture range and read actual sensor, then make a grounded recommendation.
